devanagari: श्रीमद्भागवत महापुराण । षष्ठः स्कन्धःषोडशोऽध्यायः । राजपुत्रदेहवियुक्तस्य जीवात्मन उक्तिः चित्रकेतवे नारदकर्तृकं संकर्षण । मंत्रप्रदानं ...
english_devnagari: śrīmadbhāgavata mahāpurāṇa ṣaṣṭhaḥ skandhaḥṣoḍaśo rājaputradehaviyuktasya jīvātmana uktiḥ citraketave nāradakartṛkaṃ saṃkarṣaṇa maṃtrapradānaṃ tajjapena vidyādharatva prāptirbhagavato anantasya darśanaṃ caśrīśuka uvācaatha devaṛṣī rājan saṃparetaṃ nṛpātmajam darśayitveti hovāca jñātīnāṃ anuśocatām

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Then, Oh King, the divine sage Nārada (by his yogic powers) showed the spirit of the departed prince to the relatives who were mourning and spoke to the spirit.

devanagari: श्रीनारद उवाचजीवात्मन् पश्य भद्रं ते मातरं पितरं च ते । सुहृदो बान्धवास्तप्ताः शुचा त्वत्कृतया भृशम् ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīnārada uvācajīvātman paśya bhadraṃ te mātaraṃ pitaraṃ ca te suhṛdo bāndhavāstaptāḥ śucā tvatkṛtayā bhṛśam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative plural from √ tap class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tap class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tap class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ tap class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ tap class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ tap class 4 verb]

translation:
Nārada said:
Oh Jīva, God bless you. Have a look at your mother, father, friends and relatives who are deeply afflicted with grief for you.

devanagari: कलेवरं स्वमाविश्य शेषमायुः सुहृद्वृतः । भुङ्क्ष्व भोगान् पितृप्रत्तान् अधितिष्ठ नृपासनम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: kalevaraṃ svamāviśya śeṣamāyuḥ suhṛdvṛtaḥ bhuṅkṣva bhogān pitṛprattān adhitiṣṭha nṛpāsanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ vṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ vṛ class 9 verb]

translation: Please enter this body of yours. Surrounded by your friends enjoy pleasures throughout the remaining period of your life—pleasures provided to you by your father and ascend the royal throne.

devanagari: जीव उवाचकस्मिन् जन्मन्यमी मह्यं पितरो मातरोऽभवन् । कर्मभिर्भ्राम्यमाणस्य देवतिर्यङ्नृयोनिषु ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: jīva uvācakasmin janmanyamī mahyaṃ pitaro mātaro karmabhirbhrāmyamāṇasya devatiryaṅnṛyoniṣu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]

translation:
Jīva said:
In what life were these my father and mother, while I was revolving in the cycle of births in the species of gods, subhuman beings and human beings by my karmas.

devanagari: बन्धुज्ञात्यरिमध्यस्थ मित्रोदासीनविद्विषः । सर्व एव हि सर्वेषां भवन्ति क्रमशो मिथः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: bandhujñātyarimadhyastha mitrodāsīnavidviṣaḥ sarva eva hi sarveṣāṃ bhavanti kramaśo mithaḥ
translation: For, all persons are by turns related to others as relatives, cousins, enemies, mediators, friends, indifferent men and bitter adversaries.

devanagari: यथा वस्तूनि पण्यानि हेमादीनि ततस्ततः । पर्यटन्ति नरेष्वेवं जीवो योनिषु कर्तृषु ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā vastūni paṇyāni hemādīni tatastataḥ paryaṭanti nareṣvevaṃ jīvo yoniṣu kartṛṣu

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ paṇ ], [vocative plural from √ paṇ ], [accusative plural from √ paṇ ]

translation: Just as commodities like gold and other articles change hand from one customer to another and one place to another, similarly a Jīva wanders from one species of existence to another.

devanagari: नित्यस्यार्थस्य सम्बन्धो ह्यनित्यो दृश्यते नृषु । यावद्यस्य हि सम्बन्धो ममत्वं तावदेव हि ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: nityasyārthasya sambandho hyanityo dṛśyate nṛṣu yāvadyasya hi sambandho mamatvaṃ tāvadeva hi
translation: Even during the life-time of a person, the relation even of men with animals is temporary. The attachment of mineness lasts so long as the particular relation exists.

devanagari: एवं योनिगतो जीवः स नित्यो निरहङ्कृतः । यावद् यत्रोपलभ्येत तावत् स्वत्वं हि तस्य तत् ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ yonigato jīvaḥ sa nityo nirahaṅkṛtaḥ yāvad yatropalabhyeta tāvat svatvaṃ hi tasya tat
translation: In the same way, the relation of a Jīva who has entered the womb of a particular species is transient. For, by himself, Jīva is eternal and as such unrelated to his body and is therefore without attachment (as a son etc.). The sense of belongingness lasts so long as and only with him, with whom he is associated due to his karma (and not after death. Therefore grief for me is unnecessary).

devanagari: एष नित्योऽव्ययः सूक्ष्म एष सर्वाश्रयः स्वदृक् । आत्ममायागुणैर्विश्वं आत्मानं सृजते प्रभुः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa nityo ātmamāyāguṇairviśvaṃ ātmānaṃ sṛjate prabhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: This Atman is eternal. He is not subject to decay or change. He is subtle and free from birth. He is the basic support (as the material cause of the universe) of all. He is self-illuminating. Being all-powerful he manifests himself as the universe by the guṇas of his Māyā.[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Padaratnāvalī explains: The Jīva is eternally related with Hari who by the guṇas of his Māyā creates his universe. Hari is eternally connected with all. He is eternal, not conditioned by change or decay, subtle but absolutely one. He perceives everything as he is the Lord of all. Thus the relation of a Jīva with any person is temporary.

devanagari: न ह्यस्यास्ति प्रियः कश्चित् नाप्रियः स्वः परोऽपि वा । एकः सर्वधियां द्रष्टा कर्तॄणां गुणदोषयोः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: na hyasyāsti priyaḥ kaścit nāpriyaḥ svaḥ paro ekaḥ sarvadhiyāṃ draṣṭā kartṝṇāṃ guṇadoṣayoḥ
translation: To him no one is very dear, nor anyone hateful. He has no kith or kin or an alien. He is one, the witness of the working of the minds of friends and foes (who do good or evil).

devanagari: नादत्त आत्मा हि गुणं न दोषं न क्रियाफलम् । उदासीनवदासीनः परावरदृगीश्वरः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: nādatta ātmā hi guṇaṃ na doṣaṃ na kriyāphalam udāsīnavadāsīnaḥ parāvaradṛgīśvaraḥ
translation: The Atman does not accept (experience) the good or evil (i.e. pleasure or pain) nor is he the recipient of the fruit of karmas (namely kingship). He is a dispassionate witness of cause and effect and not their enjoyer. He is thus unconditioned by body and is independent.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचइत्युदीर्य गतो जीवो ज्ञातयस्तस्य ते तदा । विस्मिता मुमुचुः शोकं छित्त्वात्म स्नेहश्रृङ्खलाम् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaityudīrya gato jīvo jñātayastasya te tadā vismitā mumucuḥ śokaṃ chittvātma snehaśrṛṅkhalām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
After explaining thus the spirit departed. The relatives of that Jīva then became wonder-struck. They cut asunder their bonds of affection to him and gave up sorrow.

devanagari: निर्हृत्य ज्ञातयो ज्ञातेः देहं कृत्वोचिताः क्रियाः । तत्यजुर्दुस्त्यजं स्नेहं शोकमोहभयार्तिदम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: nirhṛtya jñātayo jñāteḥ dehaṃ kṛtvocitāḥ kriyāḥ tatyajurdustyajaṃ snehaṃ śokamohabhayārtidam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ uc class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ uc class 4 verb]
[nominative plural from √ uc class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ uc class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ uc class 4 verb]

translation: The relatives took away the dead body and performed the suitable obsequies. They set aside their affection which is so difficult to be rid of, and which causes grief, delusion, fear and distress.

devanagari: बालघ्न्यो व्रीडितास्तत्र बालहत्याहतप्रभाः । बालहत्याव्रतं चेरुः ब्राह्मणैः यन्निरूपितम् । यमुनायां महाराज स्मरन्त्यो द्विजभाषितम् ॥ १४ ...
english_devnagari: bālaghnyo vrīḍitāstatra bālahatyāhataprabhāḥ bālahatyāvrataṃ ceruḥ brāhmaṇaiḥ yannirūpitam yamunāyāṃ mahārāja smarantyo dvijabhāṣitam

[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb]

translation: The queens who killed the child and whose charms waned due to the infanticide, became ashamed. They performed the expiation for infanticide as enunciated to them by the Brāhmaṇas on the bank of the Yamunā, remembering what the Brāhmaṇas have explained to them, Oh Mahārāja.

devanagari: स इत्थं प्रतिबुद्धात्मा चित्रकेतुर्द्विजोक्तिभिः । गृहान्धकूपान् निष्क्रान्तः सरःपङ्कादिव द्विपः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sa itthaṃ pratibuddhātmā citraketurdvijoktibhiḥ gṛhāndhakūpān niṣkrāntaḥ saraḥpaṅkādiva dvipaḥ
translation: Citraketu who was thus spiritually awakened by the words (of advice) by the Brāhmaṇa sages, came out of the dark deceptive well in the form of domestic life, just as an elephant extricates himself out of the mire in the lake.

devanagari: कालिन्द्यां विधिवत् स्नात्वा कृतपुण्यजलक्रियः । मौनेन संयतप्राणो ब्रह्मपुत्राववन्दत ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kālindyāṃ vidhivat snātvā kṛtapuṇyajalakriyaḥ maunena saṃyataprāṇo brahmaputrāvavandata
translation: Having taken ablution in the Yamunā according to the sacred precepts, and having poured out oblations of water (to the Pitṛs), he, with his senses properly controlled, bowed to the two sons of god Brahmā (viz. Nārada and Aṅgiras) insilence.

devanagari: अथ तस्मै प्रपन्नाय भक्ताय प्रयतात्मने । भगवान् नारदः प्रीतो विद्यामेतामुवाच ह ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: atha tasmai prapannāya bhaktāya prayatātmane bhagavān nāradaḥ prīto vidyāmetāmuvāca ha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]
[imperative active third dual], [imperative middle third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: The venerable sage Nārada gladly taught this great lore to the king who was their devotee and who resorted to them for protection and had fully controlled his mind.

devanagari: ॐ नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते वासुदेवाय धीमहि । प्रद्युम्नायानिरुद्धाय नमः सङ्कर्षणाय च ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: oṃ namastubhyaṃ bhagavate vāsudevāya dhīmahi pradyumnāyāniruddhāya namaḥ saṅkarṣaṇāya ca
translation: “OM! Salutation unto you, Oh glorious Lord Vāsudeva. We contemplate (mentally pay obeisance to) you. We bow to Pradyumna, Aniruddha and Saṅkarṣaṇa (all the vyūhas of the Lord).

devanagari: नमो विज्ञानमात्राय परमानन्दमूर्तये । आत्मारामाय शान्ताय निवृत्तद्वैतदृष्टये ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: namo vijñānamātrāya paramānandamūrtaye ātmārāmāya śāntāya nivṛttadvaitadṛṣṭaye

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [dative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [dative single from √ śam class 9 verb]
[dative single from √ śam class 4 verb], [dative single from √ śam class 9 verb]

translation: Obeisance to You who are pure, absolute knowledge, the very embodiment of supreme bliss, delighted with yourself, serene and above the notion of duality.

devanagari: आत्मानन्दानुभूत्यैव न्यस्तशक्त्यूर्मये नमः । हृषीकेशाय महते नमस्ते विश्वमूर्तये ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: ātmānandānubhūtyaiva nyastaśaktyūrmaye namaḥ hṛṣīkeśāya mahate namaste viśvamūrtaye
translation: Hail to you who, through realization of your blissful character, kept yourself above the waves; the feelings of love, hate etc.) caused by Māyā. Salutation to the great Hṛṣīkeśa (the Supreme Master of Senses) whose body is the universe.

devanagari: वचस्युपरतेऽप्राप्य य एको मनसा सह । अनामरूपश्चिन्मात्रः सोऽव्यान्नः सदसत्परः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: vacasyuparate anāmarūpaścinmātraḥ so

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ u class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ u class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ u class 5 verb]

translation: May he protect us—he who shines alone by himself and is beyond the reach of speech and other senses and the mind; who is pure consciousness and has no name or form and who is the cause of the law of causation (and hence above it).

devanagari: यस्मिन्निदं यतश्चेदं तिष्ठत्यप्येति जायते । मृण्मयेष्विव मृज्जातिः तस्मै ते ब्रह्मणे नमः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: yasminnidaṃ yataścedaṃ tiṣṭhatyapyeti jāyate mṛṇmayeṣviva mṛjjātiḥ tasmai te brahmaṇe namaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[ablative single from √ i class 2 verb], [genitive single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ sthā class 1 verb], [locative single from √ sthā class 1 verb]

translation: Bow to you Supreme Brahman on whom the universe (the product of cause and effect) is based, from whom it evolves and in whom it dissolves and thus pervades everything in the universe, just as products of earth do with reference to the earth.

devanagari: यन्न स्पृशन्ति न विदुः मनोबुद्धीन्द्रियासवः । अन्तर्बहिश्च विततं व्योमवत् तन्नतोऽस्म्यहम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: yanna spṛśanti na viduḥ manobuddhīndriyāsavaḥ antarbahiśca vitataṃ vyomavat tannato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb], [accusative plural from √ spṛś class 6 verb]

[nominative single from √ mū class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mū class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ mū class 1 verb]

translation: I salute the Brahman which pervades everything from within and without like the sky but to whom the Prāṇas (organs of activity) do not touch (by their conative force) nor do the mind, intellect and cognitive senses comprehend him (by their power of cognition).

devanagari: देहेन्द्रियप्राणमनोधियोऽमी । यदंशविद्धाः प्रचरन्ति कर्मसु । नैवान्यदा लौहमिवाप्रतप्तं । स्थानेषु तद्द्रष्ट्रपदेशमेति ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: dehendriyaprāṇamanodhiyo yadaṃśaviddhāḥ pracaranti karmasu naivānyadā lauhamivāprataptaṃ sthāneṣu taddraṣṭrapadeśameti

[nominative single from √ tap class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tap class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ tap class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ tap class 4 verb]

translation: It is only when these—body, senses, vital airs, mind and intellect—are charged by his part or presence (aṃśa) that these carry out their functions and not otherwise, just as an unheated piece of iron (does not emit heat or light.) What is called the ‘spectator’ is another designation of yours in the state of wakefulness (of the Jīva. He has no independent existence apart from you).

devanagari: ॐ नमो भगवते महापुरुषाय महानुभावाय । महाविभूतिपतये सकलसात्वत परिवृढनिकर । करकमल कुड्मलोपलालित । चरणारविन्दयुगल परमपरमेष्ठिन् नमस्ते ॥ २५...
english_devnagari: oṃ namo bhagavate mahāpuruṣāya mahānubhāvāya mahāvibhūtipataye sakalasātvata parivṛḍhanikara karakamala kuḍmalopalālita caraṇāravindayugala paramaparameṣṭhin namaste
translation: Salutations to you the Supreme Person, designated by the sacred symbol OM, the possessor of Supreme Might, whose lotus-like feet are adored in great love with the lotusbud-like joined palms by all the great Sātvatas (votaries of the Lord). I reverentially bow to you, the most excellent controller of all.”

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचभक्तायैतां प्रपन्नाय विद्यामादिश्य नारदः । ययावङ्गिरसा साकं धाम स्वायम्भुवं प्रभो ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācabhaktāyaitāṃ prapannāya vidyāmādiśya nāradaḥ yayāvaṅgirasā sākaṃ dhāma svāyambhuvaṃ prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having taught this prayer-lore to his devotee who sought his protection, Nārada along with Aṅgiras, went to the region of god Brahmā, Oh King.

devanagari: चित्रकेतुस्तु विद्यां तां यथा नारदभाषिताम् । धारयामास सप्ताहं अब्भक्षः सुसमाहितः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: citraketustu vidyāṃ tāṃ yathā nāradabhāṣitām dhārayāmāsa saptāhaṃ abbhakṣaḥ susamāhitaḥ
translation: Citraketu observed the course of that lore (of meditation and prayer) with perfect concentration as per instructions of Nārada. For one week he subsisted on water only.

devanagari: ततः स सप्तरात्रान्ते विद्यया धार्यमाणया । विद्याधराधिपत्यं च लेभेऽप्रतिहतं नृप ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ sa saptarātrānte vidyayā dhāryamāṇayā vidyādharādhipatyaṃ ca lebhe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Then at the end of the seventh night, the king attained to permanent overlordship of Vidyādharas through the practice of that lore.

devanagari: ततः कतिपयाहोभिः विद्ययेद्धमनोगतिः । जगाम देवदेवस्य शेषस्य चरणान्तिकम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ katipayāhobhiḥ vidyayeddhamanogatiḥ jagāma devadevasya śeṣasya caraṇāntikam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ indh class 7 verb], [accusative single from √ indh class 7 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then after some days, his mental power became illumined through the practice and repetition of the same lore, and he went near the feet of Śeṣa, the God of gods.

devanagari: मृणालगौरं शितिवाससं स्फुरत् । किरीटकेयूरकटित्रकङ्कणम् । प्रसन्नवक्त्रारुणलोचनं वृतं । ददर्श सिद्धेश्वरमण्डलैः प्रभुम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: mṛṇālagauraṃ śitivāsasaṃ sphurat kirīṭakeyūrakaṭitrakaṅkaṇam prasannavaktrāruṇalocanaṃ vṛtaṃ dadarśa siddheśvaramaṇḍalaiḥ prabhum

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ sphur class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sphur class 6 verb]

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: He saw the Lord Śeṣa white like a lotus fibre, clad in blue garments and adorned with brilliant crown, armlets, girdle and wristlets, and with gracious countenance and reddish eyes, attended upon by multitudes of great Siddhas.

devanagari: तद्दर्शनध्वस्तसमस्तकिल्बिषः । स्वस्थामलान्तःकरणोऽभ्ययान्मुनिः । प्रवृद्धभक्त्या प्रणयाश्रुलोचनः । प्रहृष्टरोमानमदादिपुरुषम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: taddarśanadhvastasamastakilbiṣaḥ svasthāmalāntaḥkaraṇo pravṛddhabhaktyā praṇayāśrulocanaḥ prahṛṣṭaromānamadādipuruṣam
translation: All his sins having been destroyed at the sight of the Lord, the sage Citraketu whose heart became cleansed of all impurities, approached the Most Ancient Person in silence. Due to surging devotion, his eyes were flowing with tears of love and his hair were standing on ends due to ecstasy, he prostrated himself before him.

devanagari: स उत्तमश्लोकपदाब्जविष्टरं । प्रेमाश्रुलेशैरुपमेहयन्मुहुः । प्रेमोपरुद्धाखिलवर्णनिर्गमो । नैवाशकत्तं प्रसमीडितुं चिरम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: sa uttamaślokapadābjaviṣṭaraṃ premāśruleśairupamehayanmuhuḥ premoparuddhākhilavarṇanirgamo naivāśakattaṃ prasamīḍituṃ ciram
translation: Repeatedly moistening with his tears of love the altar under the lotus-like feet of the glorious Lord, and his power of expression was being choked up by his overpowering love and devotion, he was not for a long time able to praise him properly.

devanagari: ततः समाधाय मनो मनीषया । बभाष एतत्प्रतिलब्धवागसौ । नियम्य सर्वेन्द्रियबाह्यवर्तनं । जगद्‍गुरुं सात्वतशास्त्रविग्रहम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ samādhāya mano manīṣayā babhāṣa etatpratilabdhavāgasau niyamya sarvendriyabāhyavartanaṃ jagad‍guruṃ sātvataśāstravigraham

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Then, composing his mind by the force of reason and having restrained the senses from going out to external objects, he addressed the following words to the father of the world, the embodiment of the Sātvata Śāstra, i.e. Pañcarātra (or whose body stood described in the Pañcarātra scriptures).

devanagari: चित्रकेतुरुवाचअजित जितः सममतिभिः । साधुभिर्भवान् जितात्मभिर्भवता । विजितास्तेऽपि च भजतां । अकामात्मनां य आत्मदोऽतिकरुणः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: citraketuruvācaajita jitaḥ samamatibhiḥ sādhubhirbhavān jitātmabhirbhavatā vijitāste akāmātmanāṃ ya ātmado

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Citraketu said:
Oh unconquered Lord! (Despite your unconquerableness) you have been vanquished by self-controlled righteous persons of impartial and unruffled minds. They, in their turn, are conquered by You. You are so very gracious that you bestow your own self upon your devotees whose minds are completely free from desires.

devanagari: तव विभवः खलु भगवन् । जगदुदयस्थितिलयादीनि । विश्वसृजस्तेंऽशांशास्तत्र । मृषा स्पर्धन्ति पृथगभिमत्या ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tava vibhavaḥ khalu bhagavan jagadudayasthitilayādīni viśvasṛjasteṃ mṛṣā spardhanti pṛthagabhimatyā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ spṛdh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ spṛdh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ spṛdh class 1 verb]

translation: Oh glorious Lord! The creation, preservation and destruction of the universe is your prowess—a sport. The (so- called) creators of the universe are merely the portion of your portions. They, however, regard themselves severally as creators and vainly vie with one another.

devanagari: परमाणुपरममहतोः । त्वमाद्यन्तान्तरवर्ती त्रयविधुरः । आदावन्तेऽपि च सत्त्वानां । यद्ध्रुवं तदेवान्तरालेऽपि ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: paramāṇuparamamahatoḥ tvamādyantāntaravartī trayavidhuraḥ ādāvante yaddhruvaṃ tadevāntarāle

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: You are at the creation, destruction as well as during the intervening period of the subtlest atom as well as (of the universe) of the largest dimensions. But you are (yourself) devoid of this triad of limitation. You are that unchanging constant who exist at the beginning and end as well as in the interim of all created things.

devanagari: क्षित्यादिभिरेष किलावृतः । सप्तभिर्दशगुणोत्तरैरण्डकोशः । यत्र पतत्यणुकल्पः । सहाण्डकोटिकोटिभिस्तदनन्तः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: kṣityādibhireṣa kilāvṛtaḥ saptabhirdaśaguṇottarairaṇḍakośaḥ yatra patatyaṇukalpaḥ sahāṇḍakoṭikoṭibhistadanantaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [vocative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [accusative dual from √ pat class 1 verb], [locative single from √ pat class 1 verb]

translation: The egg of the universe is enveloped by seven sheaths of the earth and other elements etc., each outer envelop being ten times the extent of the enclosure inner to it. This egg-like universe together with millions and millions of other universes appears just like an atom on your head. Hence, you are called Ananta, the infinite.

devanagari: विषयतृषो नरपशवो । य उपासते विभूतीर्न परं त्वाम् । तेषामाशिष ईश तदनु । विनश्यन्ति यथा राजकुलम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣayatṛṣo narapaśavo ya upāsate vibhūtīrna paraṃ tvām teṣāmāśiṣa īśa tadanu vinaśyanti yathā rājakulam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ śā class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ śā class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ śā class 4 verb]

translation: Oh Controller of the universe! Those persons are really beasts who, in their thirst for objects of pleasure propitiate the deities concerned (like Indra, who are but your parts only) and not you—for, the blessings (so conferred upon them) come to an end with the extinction of the deities, just as the good fortunes of the followers of a king perish with the fall of that royal house.

devanagari: कामधियस्त्वयि रचिता । न परम रोहन्ति यथा करम्भबीजानि । ज्ञानात्मन्यगुणमये । गुणगणतोऽस्य द्वन्द्वजालानि ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kāmadhiyastvayi racitā na parama rohanti yathā karambhabījāni jñānātmanyaguṇamaye guṇagaṇato

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ruh class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ruh class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ruh class 1 verb]

translation: Oh Supreme Lord! Even if worldly desires are entreated of you who are pure knowledge and consciousness and not a product of guṇas, they do not grow like the fried seeds (which cannot sprout forth). For all the pairs of opposites (e.g. love—hate, leading to rebirth) are the result of guṇas (and you are above them).

devanagari: जितमजित तदा भवता । यदाह भागवतं धर्ममनवद्यम् । निष्किञ्चना ये मुनय । आत्मारामा यमुपासतेऽपवर्गाय ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: jitamajita tadā bhavatā yadāha bhāgavataṃ dharmamanavadyam niṣkiñcanā ye munaya ātmārāmā yamupāsate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ji class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ji class 9 verb], [accusative single from √ ji class 9 verb]

translation: Oh unconquerable one! When you propounded the pure Bhāgavata Dharma (the righteous path leading to the realization of the glorious Lord), you have conquered all (made all your own). For sinless sages (like Sanatkumāra) who are possessionless and free of desires and find delight in the Supreme Soul, resort to that Dharma (or to you) for Liberation.

devanagari: विषममतिर्न यत्र नृणां । त्वमहमिति मम तवेति च यदन्यत्र । विषमधिया रचितो यः । स ह्यविशुद्धः क्षयिष्णुरधर्मबहुलः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣamamatirna yatra nṛṇāṃ tvamahamiti mama taveti ca yadanyatra viṣamadhiyā racito yaḥ sa hyaviśuddhaḥ kṣayiṣṇuradharmabahulaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Here in the Bhāgavata Dharma men do not cherish crooked, differential notions such as ‘I’, ‘you’, ‘mine’ and ‘yours’, (as is in other cults, as that of sacrifice). Whatever (act) is performed with the notion of such differences is impure, perishable and full of sin, (like hiṃsā).

devanagari: कः क्षेमो निजपरयोः । कियान्वार्थः स्वपरद्रुहा धर्मेण । स्वद्रोहात्तव कोपः । परसम्पीडया च तथाधर्मः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: kaḥ kṣemo nijaparayoḥ kiyānvārthaḥ svaparadruhā dharmeṇa svadrohāttava kopaḥ parasampīḍayā ca tathādharmaḥ
translation: What good to one’s own self or to another can accrue from a dharma (path of religion) or what benefit can be gained through a dharma which is harmful to oneself and to others. It is irreligion, for pain is caused to oneself through self-mortification and sin is committed by offending others.

devanagari: न व्यभिचरति तवेक्षा । यया ह्यभिहितो भागवतो धर्मः । स्थिरचरसत्त्वकदम्बेष्व । पृथग्धियो यमुपासते त्वार्याः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: na vyabhicarati tavekṣā yayā hyabhihito bhāgavato dharmaḥ sthiracarasattvakadambeṣva pṛthagdhiyo yamupāsate tvāryāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ tvar class 1 verb]

translation: Your vision by which you expounded the Bhāgavata-dharma never goes astray from reality. Hence, the noble Souls who do not regard the multitudes of the mobile and immobile being as different from you follow that dharma, Oh Lord.

devanagari: न हि भगवन्नघटितमिदं । त्वद्दर्शनान् नृणामखिलपापक्षयः । यन्नाम सकृच्छ्रवणात् । पुल्कसकोऽपि विमुच्यते संसारात् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: na hi bhagavannaghaṭitamidaṃ tvaddarśanān nṛṇāmakhilapāpakṣayaḥ yannāma sakṛcchravaṇāt pulkasako

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ ghaṭ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ghaṭ ]
[nominative single from √ ghaṭ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ ghaṭ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ ghaṭ ], [accusative single from √ ghaṭ ]

translation: It is no miracle that all the sins committed by men are annihilated at your sight, for even an outcaste like a Cāṇḍāla becomes liberated from the cycle of birth and death, by hearing your name once only, Oh glorious Lord.

devanagari: अथ भगवन् वयमधुना । त्वदवलोकपरिमृष्टाशयमलाः । सुरऋषिणा यदुदितं । तावकेन कथमन्यथा भवति ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: atha bhagavan vayamadhunā tvadavalokaparimṛṣṭāśayamalāḥ suraṛṣiṇā yaduditaṃ tāvakena kathamanyathā bhavati
translation: Oh Lord, we have got our heart cleansed of all the impurities by your very sight. How could that which the divine sage, your own devotee, has said, turn out to be untrue?

devanagari: विदितमनन्त समस्तं । तव जगदात्मनो जनैरिहाचरितम् । विज्ञाप्यं परमगुरोः । कियदिव सवितुरिव खद्योतैः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: viditamananta samastaṃ tava jagadātmano janairihācaritam vijñāpyaṃ paramaguroḥ kiyadiva savituriva khadyotaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vid class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vid class 2 verb]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Oh Infinite Lord! As you are the Soul of the world, you know everything that is done in this world by people. You are the Creator and Preceptor of the world. There is nothing new to be specially made known to you, as there is nothing to be revealed to the Sun by the fire-flies.

devanagari: नमस्तुभ्यं भगवते । सकलजगत्स्थितिलयोदयेशाय । दुरवसितात्मगतये । कुयोगिनां भिदा परमहंसाय ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: namastubhyaṃ bhagavate sakalajagatsthitilayodayeśāya duravasitātmagataye kuyogināṃ bhidā paramahaṃsāya
translation: Salutations to you, Oh glorious Lord, who are the master and controller of the creation, sustenance and destruction of all the worlds. You are the purest of the pure (Paramahaṃsa). Your real nature is incomprehensible to bad yogins who entertain the notion of difference.

devanagari: यं वै श्वसन्तमनु विश्वसृजः श्वसन्ति । यं चेकितानमनु चित्तय उच्चकन्ति । भूमण्डलं सर्षपायति यस्य मूर्ध्नि । तस्मै नमो भगवतेऽस्तु सहस्रमूर...
english_devnagari: yaṃ vai śvasantamanu viśvasṛjaḥ śvasanti yaṃ cekitānamanu cittaya uccakanti bhūmaṇḍalaṃ sarṣapāyati yasya mūrdhni tasmai namo bhagavate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]
[nominative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb], [accusative plural from √ śvas class 2 verb]

translation: The creators of the world (e.g. god Brahmā and other gods who preside over the conative organs) (are activated) when you breathe (become active). When you (wish to) see, the gods (like the Sun who preside over the cognitive organs,) are able to get perception. The globe of this earth is (insignificant) like a mustard seed on your head. My Salutations to you, Oh glorious Lord of a thousand heads.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचसंस्तुतो भगवान् एवं अनन्तस्तमभाषत । विद्याधरपतिं प्रीतः चित्रकेतुं कुरूद्वह ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācasaṃstuto bhagavān evaṃ anantastamabhāṣata vidyādharapatiṃ prītaḥ citraketuṃ kurūdvaha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Lord Ananta who was thus eulogised, was pleased with him. He spoke out to Citraketu the king of Vidyādharas, Oh scion of the Kuru family (Parīkṣit).

devanagari: श्रीभगवान् उवाचयन्नारदाङ्गिरोभ्यां ते व्याहृतं मेऽनुशासनम् । संसिद्धोऽसि तया राजन् विद्यया दर्शनाच्च मे ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavān uvācayannāradāṅgirobhyāṃ te vyāhṛtaṃ me saṃsiddho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]

translation:
The glorious Lord said:
Oh King, it is by the precept (of the means of propitiating me) taught to you by Nārada and Aṅgiras and by the practice of that spiritual lore and by my sight that you have fully achieved your purpose.

devanagari: अहं वै सर्वभूतानि भूतात्मा भूतभावनः । शब्दब्रह्म परं ब्रह्म ममोभे शाश्वती तनू ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: ahaṃ vai sarvabhūtāni bhūtātmā bhūtabhāvanaḥ śabdabrahma paraṃ brahma mamobhe śāśvatī tanū

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: I am verily all the created beings, their Soul and their support. I am the Veda the Supreme Brahma and both of these are my eternal form.

devanagari: लोके विततमात्मानं लोकं चात्मनि सन्ततम् । उभयं च मया व्याप्तं मयि चैवोभयं कृतम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: loke vitatamātmānaṃ lokaṃ cātmani santatam ubhayaṃ ca mayā vyāptaṃ mayi caivobhayaṃ kṛtam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: One should understand that his own Self is pervading the whole of the universe (as the subject of experience) and that the universe is resting on the Ātman (i.e. is superimposed on the Soul) and that both (one’s own Self and the universe) are pervaded by me (as the prime cause).

devanagari: यथा सुषुप्तः पुरुषो विश्वं पश्यति चात्मनि । आत्मानमेकदेशस्थं मन्यते स्वप्न उत्थितः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: yathā suṣuptaḥ puruṣo viśvaṃ paśyati cātmani ātmānamekadeśasthaṃ manyate svapna utthitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle third single], [present passive third single]

translation: Just as in a state of profound slumber, one sees the universe (i.e. the various objects in it) in his own Self, and waking up from the dream, he finds himself alone and in a particular place (of sleeping such as a bed-room).

devanagari: एवं जागरणादीनि जीवस्थानानि चात्मनः । मायामात्राणि विज्ञाय तद्द्रष्टारं परं स्मरेत् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ jāgaraṇādīni jīvasthānāni cātmanaḥ māyāmātrāṇi vijñāya taddraṣṭāraṃ paraṃ smaret
translation: Similarly wakefulness etc. are the conditions of Jīva and are the products of Māyā with reference to Ātman. Therefore, one should remember that Paramātman is only a witness.

devanagari: येन प्रसुप्तः पुरुषः स्वापं वेदात्मनस्तदा । सुखं च निर्गुणं ब्रह्म तमात्मानमवेहि माम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: yena prasuptaḥ puruṣaḥ svāpaṃ vedātmanastadā sukhaṃ ca nirguṇaṃ brahma tamātmānamavehi mām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Recognize me to be that Atman or Brahman through whom the Jīva in a sleeping condition, regards himself as in deep sleep. I am absolute bliss beyond the range of guṇas or the senses.

devanagari: उभयं स्मरतः पुंसः प्रस्वापप्रतिबोधयोः । अन्वेति व्यतिरिच्येत तज्ज्ञानं ब्रह्म तत्परम् ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: ubhayaṃ smarataḥ puṃsaḥ prasvāpapratibodhayoḥ anveti vyatiricyeta tajjñānaṃ brahma tatparam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative plural from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [ablative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[ablative single from √ smṛ class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ smṛ class 1 verb]
[optative middle third single], [optative passive third single]

translation: That knowledge is the Supreme Brahma—knowledge which is found in a person who remembers his experiences during sleep and wakefulness—a connecting link as well as the differentia between the two states of experience.

devanagari: यदेतद् विस्मृतं पुंसो मद्‍भावं भिन्नमात्मनः । ततः संसार एतस्य देहाद्देहो मृतेर्मृतिः ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: yadetad vismṛtaṃ puṃso mad‍bhāvaṃ bhinnamātmanaḥ tataḥ saṃsāra etasya dehāddeho mṛtermṛtiḥ
translation: When the Jīva forgets my real nature, he regards himself as different from me and this is the cause of his Saṃsāra, in which he goes from birth after birth and suffers death after death.

devanagari: लब्ध्वेह मानुषीं योनिं ज्ञानविज्ञानसम्भवाम् । आत्मानं यो न बुद्ध्येत न क्वचिन् शममाप्नुयात् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: labdhveha mānuṣīṃ yoniṃ jñānavijñānasambhavām ātmānaṃ yo na buddhyeta na kvacin śamamāpnuyāt
translation: Having attained birth in the human species here, where the knowledge of the Self (as detailed in scriptures) and the wisdom, i.e. direct knowledge and perception of the Self, can be developed, he who does not realize me, can never find peace and happiness.

devanagari: स्मृत्वेहायां परिक्लेशं ततः फलविपर्ययम् । अभयं चाप्यनीहायां सङ्कल्पाद् विरमेत्कविः ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: smṛtvehāyāṃ parikleśaṃ tataḥ phalaviparyayam abhayaṃ cāpyanīhāyāṃ saṅkalpād virametkaviḥ
translation: Remembering that in the Pravṛtti-mārga (i.e. the path of karma) there are great afflictions and contrary results (of the expected fruit), and there is fearlessness, i.e. Liberation (Mokṣa) in the Nivṛtti-mārga (the path of cessation of activities), a wise man should desist from the path of karma.

devanagari: सुखाय दुःखमोक्षाय कुर्वाते दम्पती क्रियाः । ततोऽनिवृत्तिः अप्राप्तिः दुखस्य च सुखस्य च ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: sukhāya duḥkhamokṣāya kurvāte dampatī kriyāḥ tato
translation: A pair of husband and wife perform (ritualistic) acts for getting pleasure and avoidance of misery. But there is no escape from misery nor the attainment of happiness, as a result of ritualistic performance.

devanagari: एवं विपर्ययं बुद्ध्वा नृणां विज्ञाभिमानिनाम् । आत्मनश्च गतिं सूक्ष्मां स्थानत्रयविलक्षणाम् ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ viparyayaṃ buddhvā nṛṇāṃ vijñābhimāninām ātmanaśca gatiṃ sūkṣmāṃ sthānatrayavilakṣaṇām
translation: Having thus realized that the consequences go contrary to expectation of persons who in their pride regard themselves to be clever, and having realized the subtle nature of the Ātman which transcends the three states (viz. state of awakeness, dream and deep sleep), and being completely free (from the desire) for the pleasures of this world and the next, through one’s power of discrimination and finding satisfaction and pleasure in one’s knowledge and wisdom, a person should devote himself to me (completely).

devanagari: दृष्टश्रुताभिर्मात्राभिः निर्मुक्तः स्वेन तेजसा । ज्ञानविज्ञानसन्तृप्तो मद्‍भक्तः पुरुषो भवेत् ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭaśrutābhirmātrābhiḥ nirmuktaḥ svena tejasā jñānavijñānasantṛpto mad‍bhaktaḥ puruṣo bhavet
translation: Having thus realized that the consequences go contrary to expectation of persons who in their pride regard themselves to be clever, and having realized the subtle nature of the Ātman which transcends the three states (viz. state of awakeness, dream and deep sleep), and being completely free (from the desire) for the pleasures of this world and the next, through one’s power of discrimination and finding satisfaction and pleasure in one’s knowledge and wisdom, a person should devote himself to me (completely).

devanagari: एतावानेव मनुजैः योगनैपुण्यबुद्धिभिः । स्वार्थः सर्वात्मना ज्ञेयो यत्परात्मैकदर्शनम् ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvāneva manujaiḥ yoganaipuṇyabuddhibhiḥ svārthaḥ sarvātmanā jñeyo yatparātmaikadarśanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ jñā class 3 verb], [nominative single from √ jñā class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Realization of the unity of the individual spirit and the Supreme Soul is the real objective to be attained through all possible means by yogins whose intellect and power have acquired acuteness through yogic meditation.

devanagari: त्वमेतच्छ्रद्धया राजन् अप्रमत्तो वचो मम । ज्ञानविज्ञानसम्पन्नो धारयन्नाशु सिध्यसि ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: tvametacchraddhayā rājan apramatto vaco mama jñānavijñānasampanno dhārayannāśu sidhyasi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ rāj class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ rāj class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ dhṛ ], [vocative single from √ dhṛ ]

translation: If you bear in mind my advice and bring that into practice carefully, you will soon be blessed with knowledge and wisdom and attain Liberation, Oh King.

devanagari: श्रीशुक उवाचआश्वास्य भगवानित्थं चित्रकेतुं जगद्‍गुरुः । पश्यतस्तस्य विश्वात्मा ततश्चान्तर्दधे हरिः ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: śrīśuka uvācaāśvāsya bhagavānitthaṃ citraketuṃ jagad‍guruḥ paśyatastasya viśvātmā tataścāntardadhe hariḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
Having comforted Citraketu in this way, the glorious Lord Hari, the father and the preceptor of the world, and the Soul of the universe, immediately disappeared there while Citraketu was looking on.
